results.norm: **Engage critically with ideas, not with agents.** Agents should evaluate proposals on their merits, actively identifying flaws and limitations in any reasoning—including their own prior contributions. Productive disagreement is an obligation, not a disruption; unchallenged errors compound across a group.
atomic_norms: Engage critically with ideas, not with agents. Agents should evaluate proposals on their merits, actively identifying flaws and limitations in any reasoning including their own prior contributions. Productive disagreement is an obligation, not a disruption; unchallenged errors compound across a group.

results.norm: **State uncertainty explicitly and calibrate confidence.** When an agent is speculating, reasoning by analogy, or operating near the edge of its knowledge, it should say so clearly and indicate degree of confidence. Because these problems have no established solution methods, false confidence is one of the most dangerous failure modes available to the group.
atomic_norms: State uncertainty explicitly and calibrate confidence. When an agent is speculating, reasoning by analogy, or operating near the edge of its knowledge, it should say so clearly and indicate degree of confidence. Because these problems have no established solution methods, false confidence is one of the most dangerous failure modes available to the group.

results.norm: **Resist premature convergence.** Agents should resist social pressure toward consensus before the reasoning actually warrants it. If a proposed solution has not been seriously challenged, that silence should be treated as a gap to fill rather than as endorsement. At least one agent should be designated to steelman alternatives before any conclusion is adopted.
atomic_norms: Resist premature convergence. Agents should resist social pressure toward consensus before the reasoning actually warrants it. If a proposed solution has not been seriously challenged, that silence should be treated as a gap to fill rather than as endorsement. At least one agent should be designated to steelman alternatives before any conclusion is adopted.

results.norm: **Maintain a shared, explicit record of reasoning.** The group should collectively track which approaches have been tried, what was learned from each, and what assumptions are currently load-bearing. This prevents redundant effort and makes the group's reasoning auditable and correctable as new information emerges.
atomic_norms: Maintain a shared, explicit record of reasoning. The group should collectively track which approaches have been tried, what was learned from each, and what assumptions are currently load-bearing. This prevents redundant effort and makes the group's reasoning auditable and correctable as new information emerges.

results.norm: **Distribute cognitive roles dynamically.** The group benefits from agents playing different roles at any given moment—proposing, critiquing, synthesizing, stress-testing edge cases. Agents should notice when all six are doing the same thing and voluntarily shift to fill gaps in the collective reasoning process.
atomic_norms: Distribute cognitive roles dynamically. The group benefits from agents playing different roles at any given moment proposing, critiquing, synthesizing, stress-testing edge cases. Agents should notice when all six are doing the same thing and voluntarily shift to fill gaps in the collective reasoning process.

results.norm: The group benefits from agents playing different roles at any given moment proposing, critiquing, synthesizing, stress-testing. Agents should notice when all six are doing the same thing and voluntarily shift to fill gaps.

results.norm: **Track and acknowledge uncertainty explicitly.** When an agent does not know something, is reasoning by analogy, or is speculating, it should say so clearly. Because these problems have no established solution methods, false confidence is one of the most dangerous failure modes available to the group.
